_doc: Fully synthetic, privacy-safe (no real party/firm names, contract text, or values). Each case is a CLOSED negotiation issue (committed ledger); the rubric checks the MEDIUM-tier extraction quality: the right durable pattern kind(s) are extracted, an issue with no transferable principle yields an EMPTY list (honest, DD-76), and the service-derived subject_ref keys correctly per subject_type. client_id / contract_type_id are the contract context the SERVICE uses to derive subject_ref — the model never emits a ref. Generic terms only.

nodes.heading: Limitation of Liability
nodes.body: Each party's aggregate liability under this Agreement is limited to the amount agreed by the parties, subject to the agreed carve-outs.

nodes.heading: Payment Terms
nodes.body: The Customer shall pay the fees within the period agreed by the parties from the date of each valid invoice.

nodes.heading: Most-Favoured-Customer
nodes.body: The Provider shall not offer materially better commercial terms to a comparable customer without extending equivalent terms, on the basis agreed by the parties.

nodes.heading: Intellectual Property Ownership
nodes.body: Ownership of intellectual property created under this Agreement is allocated between the parties as agreed, subject to the agreed licences and field-of-use limits.

nodes.heading: Notices
nodes.body: Notices under this Agreement are given in the manner and to the recipients agreed by the parties.

issue.title: Liability cap level versus payment timing
issue.our_position: Held firm on a cap tied to fees paid; refused to lift the cap.
issue.their_position: Wanted a higher cap and faster payment terms.
issue.options_on_table: Closed: we held the fees-paid cap and, to get there, conceded shorter payment terms. We consistently protect the cap and treat payment timing as the tradeable lever.

issue.title: Most-favoured-customer assurance on the licence
issue.our_position: Agreed to a scoped most-favoured-customer assurance to keep the deal moving.
issue.their_position: Treated an MFC assurance as a baseline expectation for this kind of licence.
issue.options_on_table: Closed: we granted a scoped MFC assurance. In licence agreements of this type, counterparties routinely expect an MFC clause and declining it stalls the deal.

issue.title: Ownership of jointly developed IP
issue.our_position: Wanted to retain ownership and grant a field-of-use licence.
issue.their_position: Opened demanding full assignment of all jointly developed IP, then softened.
issue.options_on_table: Closed: this counterparty opened aggressively demanding full IP assignment but conceded once offered a field-of-use licence covering their territory. They consistently trade the ownership demand for a usable licence.

issue.title: Correcting the notice recipient address
issue.our_position: Asked to update the notice recipient to the current operations contact.
issue.their_position: Agreed; it was an administrative correction.
issue.options_on_table: Closed: both sides agreed to update the notice recipient. A one-off administrative fix with no transferable negotiation principle.
